operating_state: Total revenues declined to $189.0 billion from $202.8 billion driven by lower commodity prices partially offset by Hess acquisition contributing from July 18 close. Upstream earnings decreased to $12.8 billion from $18.6 billion while downstream earnings improved to $3.0 billion from $1.7 billion on better refining margins. Net capitalized costs increased to $180.0 billion from $109.3 billion reflecting substantial capital investments and Hess acquisition impact.
cost_structure: Total costs decreased to $169.3 billion from $175.3 billion primarily from lower purchased crude costs. Operating expenses increased while depreciation surged to $20.1 billion from $17.3 billion reflecting Hess acquisition and higher asset base. Production expenses increased including one-time charges for Hess severance, legal reserves, and pension settlements. Financing costs increased to $1.39 billion from $773 million due to assumed Hess debt.
material_events: Completed $48 billion Hess acquisition on July 18, issuing 301.25 million shares and assuming $8.8 billion debt, adding Guyana, Bakken, and Malaysia operations plus Hess Midstream ownership. Acquisition added 835 million barrels proved reserves increasing total to 10.6 billion BOE. Maintained quarterly dividends at $1.71 per share and completed $12.2 billion share repurchases. Recorded $131 million accrual for Louisiana coastal erosion litigation following adverse jury verdict.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency effects resulted in negative $469 million impact versus positive $520 million prior year reflecting dollar strength pressures on international operations. Effective tax rate increased from 35.5% to 36.8%. Company faces 34 climate change lawsuits with Louisiana coastal erosion exposure intensified by $744.6 million jury verdict. Commodity price volatility affects expanded global production base.
forward_outlook: Expected pension contributions of $625 million in 2026 with long-term debt maturities requiring refinancing planning. Invested $7.1 billion in producing activities with 2.8 billion BOE classified as proved undeveloped requiring future development investment. Management expects substantial settlement of restructuring liability by end of 2026.
uncertainty_profile: Environmental reserves totaled $1.06 billion with joint and several liability exposure while asset retirement obligations increased to $15.0 billion adding $1.7 billion from Hess acquisition. Unrecognized tax benefits totaled $2.6 billion with 81% potentially impacting effective tax rate, while company maintains $26.9 billion valuation allowance against deferred tax assets for foreign tax credit carryforwards expiring 2026-2035.
